text: Managing a team effectively requires several key skills. First, clear communication is essential to ensure everyone understands their tasks and goals. Delegation is also important, as it involves assigning tasks based on team members’ strengths, which boosts productivity. Regular feedback helps team members improve and feel valued; it should be constructive and specific. Motivation can be fostered by recognizing achievements and creating a positive work environment. Finally, conflicts may arise, and resolving them quickly through open discussion prevents them from affecting teamwork. By combining these practices, a manager can build a cohesive, high-performing team.
